meaning: n.闃叉椽鐭冲牑,闃叉椽宀稿
examples: During Katrina, the storm surge coming up the Intracoastal Waterway destroyed a 4, 000-foot long floodwall.
Those errors led to levee and floodwall collapses that destroyed much of New Orleans when Katrina hit seven years ago.
Days ago bused-in prison inmates worked shoulder to shoulder with the National Guard and local volunteers to build a makeshift floodwall of sand and gravel.
